explicit_text: On September 11, 2001, I was giving a presentation at the Old Post Office Tower when I first heard about an incident in New York City. Throughout the morning, the scale and scope of the attacks became clearer, including the Pentagon incident and the collapse of one of the towers. We quickly assembled staff at Survey Lodge, accounted for everyone, and maintained communication with headquarters at 900 Ohio Drive. Despite the chaos and uncertainty, our team remained calm and orderly as we managed evacuations and ensured employees could contact their families.

explicit_text: The events of September 11, 2001, were a national crisis that affected the entire United States, including Washington, D.C., New York City, and other locations like the Pentagon and Pennsylvania. From my position in D.C., I witnessed how the country responded with emergency operations, evacuations, and a collective effort to maintain calm and order. The scale of the attacks and their impact on the United States was unprecedented and deeply felt by everyone involved.
